channel.title: Triathlon on YiSheng's secret garden
channel.description: Recent content in Triathlon on YiSheng's secret garden

item.title: My First Triathlon: Graduation Is Not the End, But a New Beginning
item.description:
slug: My-First-Triathlon-Graduation-Is-Not-the-End-But-a-New-Beginning

🏅 My First Triathlon: Graduation Is Not the End, But a New Beginning

by Ezra Wu | Jul 4, 2025

That was a 1 hour and 40 minutes challenge.

I participated in a triathlon for the first time in my life—running and rafting—on a hilly course. Halfway through, my body refused to cooperate, leaving only my willpower to push me forward.

Along the way, there were thoughtful aid stations with slices of watermelon. It was so hot and I was so thirsty that I accidentally ate too much, and ended up “vomiting watermelon juice” while running—both embarrassing and funny. Every step felt like a battle with myself, and in my mind, I even imagined my loved one running ahead, just to keep me going.
